shared_model_schema:
MODEL: Contoso-lite (star schema, Power BI Tabular).

FACT TABLE 'Sales':
  Sales[SalesKey] (int, unique)
  Sales[OrderDateKey] (int)   -- relates to 'Date'[DateKey]
  Sales[ProductKey] (int)     -- relates to 'Product'[ProductKey]
  Sales[CustomerKey] (int)    -- relates to 'Customer'[CustomerKey]
  Sales[Quantity] (int)
  Sales[NetPrice] (decimal)
  Sales[NetAmount] (decimal)  -- already = Quantity * NetPrice
  Sales[ReturnFlag] (int, 0/1)

DIMENSION 'Date' (MARKED as Date table, one contiguous row per day):
  'Date'[DateKey] (int)
  'Date'[Date] (date)
  'Date'[Year] (int)
  'Date'[Month] (text)
  'Date'[MonthNumber] (int)

DIMENSION 'Product':
  'Product'[ProductKey] (int)
  'Product'[Product] (text)
  'Product'[Subcategory] (text)
  'Product'[Category] (text)
  'Product'[Color] (text)

DIMENSION 'Customer':
  'Customer'[CustomerKey] (int)
  'Customer'[Customer] (text)
  'Customer'[Country] (text)

RELATIONSHIPS (active, single direction, one-to-many from dim to Sales):
  Sales[OrderDateKey] -> 'Date'[DateKey]
  Sales[ProductKey]   -> 'Product'[ProductKey]
  Sales[CustomerKey]  -> 'Customer'[CustomerKey]

EXISTING MEASURES:
  [Total Sales] = SUM ( Sales[NetAmount] )

evals.name: generate-yoy-growth
evals.prompt:
Using the model below, write a Power BI measure that shows year-over-year sales growth as a percentage (this year vs the same period last year). Give me the measure and a short explanation.

<MODEL_SCHEMA>
evals.expected_output: A measure named e.g. [Sales YoY %] that builds on [Total Sales], shifts the date filter via SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]) (or DATEADD), uses VAR to capture current and prior values, and uses DIVIDE for the percentage. References only columns/measures that exist in the schema. Mentions it relies on the marked Date table.

evals.name: optimize-slow-measure
evals.prompt:
This measure is slow and I think it can be cleaner. Optimize it and tell me what you changed and why. Use the model schema below.

Existing measure:
US Sales Ratio =
IF (
    CALCULATE ( SUM ( Sales[NetAmount] ), FILTER ( Sales, RELATED ( 'Customer'[Country] ) = "United States" ) ) > 0,
    CALCULATE ( SUM ( Sales[Quantity] ), FILTER ( Sales, RELATED ( 'Customer'[Country] ) = "United States" ) )
        / CALCULATE ( SUM ( Sales[NetAmount] ), FILTER ( Sales, RELATED ( 'Customer'[Country] ) = "United States" ) ),
    BLANK ()
)

<MODEL_SCHEMA>
evals.expected_output: An optimized measure that: replaces the whole-table FILTER(Sales, RELATED(...)) with a column predicate 'Customer'[Country] = "United States"; replaces the / division and the IF guard with DIVIDE; stores the repeated CALCULATE subexpressions in VARs so they are computed once; plus a short list of what changed and why each is faster/safer. Only references existing columns.

evals.name: generate-pct-of-category
evals.prompt:
I want a measure that shows each product's sales as a percentage of its product category total, and it should still respect whatever slicers the user has applied (year, country, etc.). Write it using the model below and explain briefly.

<MODEL_SCHEMA>
evals.expected_output: A measure using DIVIDE([Total Sales], CALCULATE([Total Sales], REMOVEFILTERS('Product'[Product], 'Product'[Subcategory]))) or equivalent that clears only the product-level filters within the category (keeps Category and other slicers like Year/Country). Uses real columns only. Explains that REMOVEFILTERS clears the product grouping while other slicers remain in the filter context.
